Title: IWS - Verify device appears in Configurator via Q-SYS Discovery
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is powered on and connected to the same network as the Q-SYS Core.
2. Review the discovered devices list.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device automatically appears in the Configurator device list via Q-SYS Discovery.

Title: 2. The device shows its correct model identifier, IP address, and status.
3. No manual scan or discovery initiation is required.
IWS - Verify device connects when inventory name matches device name
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas (creating an inventory item).
4. IWS device is on the network and visible in Configurator.
5. Secured Communications is NOT enabled on the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Set the IWS inventory item name to match the device name shown in Configurator.

Title: 2. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).
3. Observe the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device automatically connects to the component.

Title: 2. The device reaches "OK" state without any credential entry.
3. Sensor data begins flowing to the component.
IWS - Verify device does not connect when inventory name does not match
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is on the network and visible in Configurator.
5. Secured Communications is NOT enabled on the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Set the IWS inventory item name to a value that does NOT match any device name in Configurator.

Title: 2. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).
3. Observe the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS component does not connect to any device.

Title: 2. The component shows a disconnected/unassigned state.
3. No sensor data flows.
4. No crash or unhandled error occurs.
IWS - Verify device connects with Secured Communications enabled
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is on the network and visible in Configurator.
5. Secured Communications IS enabled on the Core.
6. Device password has been set via QPM.
Preconditions: 1. Set the IWS inventory item name to match the device name in Configurator.

Title: 2. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).
3. Observe the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device connects to the component without any credential prompt in Designer.

Title: 2. The device reaches "OK" state.
3. Sensor data begins flowing to the component.
4. No authentication dialogs appear in Designer.
IWS - Verify device fails to connect with Secured Communications enabled and no password set
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is on the network and visible in Configurator.
5. Secured Communications IS enabled on the Core.
6. Device password has NOT been set via QPM.
Preconditions: 1. Set the IWS inventory item name to match the device name in Configurator.

Title: 2. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).
3. Observe the device status.
2. A fault status is displayed indicating the device is not trusted or not configured for secure communication.
3. The component does not receive sensor data.
4. No crash or unhandled exception occurs.
IWS - Verify firmware version displayed in QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is on the network and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the IWS device in QPM.

Title: 2. Locate the firmware version information.
Preconditions: 1. The current firmware version of the IWS device is displayed.

Title: 2. The version string is in a recognizable format.
IWS - Verify firmware is automatically updated through the Core
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network and connected.
4. The IWS device is running an older firmware version than what the Core provides.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).

Title: 2. Observe the IWS device status during and after deployment.
3. Wait for the firmware update to complete.
Preconditions: 1. The Core automatically pushes the firmware update to the IWS device.

Title: 2. The firmware update completes without manual intervention.
3. The device firmware version updates to the version provided by the Core.
4. The device reconnects and reaches "OK" state after the update.
IWS - Verify no firmware update when device is already up to date
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network and connected.
4. The IWS device is already running the firmware version provided by the Core.
Preconditions: 1. Deploy the design to the Core (F5).

Title: 2. Observe the IWS device status during and after deployment.
2. The device reaches "OK" state without a reboot or update cycle.
3. The device connects in the normal expected timeframe.
IWS - Verify device status indicators across Designer, Configurator, and QPM
2. Create a new design with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is on the network, name matches inventory item.
4. Design is deployed to the Core and device is connected.
Preconditions: 1. Double-click the IWS component to open Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".

Title: 2. Select the Status tab and review the device status.
3. Open Menu Bar > Tools > Configurator and locate the IWS device.
4. Open QPM in a web browser, navigate to the IWS device, and review the status at the top of the page.
Preconditions: 1. The component Status tab shows device is connected and healthy.

Title: 2. In Configurator, the IWS device shows a green box indicating the device is running normally and reachable (able to communicate with device and available to add to a design if not already in use).
3. In QPM, the top of the page displays "Status:" with a healthy/connected state.
4. Device name, model, and IP address are visible in each location.
IWS - Verify device status when device goes offline
2. Design deployed to Core with an IWS component.
3. IWS device is connected and in "OK" state.
Preconditions: 1. Verify the IWS device is in "OK" state on the component Status tab.

Title: 2. Disconnect the IWS device from the network (unplug Ethernet).
3. Wait for the status to update.
4. Check the component Status tab in Designer.
5. Check Configurator for the device.
6. Attempt to access the device in QPM via its IP address.
Preconditions: 1. The component Status tab shows a fault/disconnected state.

Title: 2. In Configurator, the IWS device no longer appears (device not visible when offline).
3. QPM does not load for the device (web interface unreachable since it is accessed via IP).
4. The status update occurs within a reasonable timeout period.
IWS - Verify device status when device comes back online
2. Design deployed to Core with an IWS component.
3. IWS device was previously connected but is currently disconnected from network.
Preconditions: 1. Verify the IWS device shows a fault/disconnected state.

Title: 2. Reconnect the IWS device to the network (plug in Ethernet).
3. Wait for the status to update.
4. Check the component Status tab in Designer.
5. Check the device in Configurator.
6. Check the device in QPM.
Preconditions: 1. The component Status tab returns to a healthy/connected state ("OK").

Title: 2. In Configurator, the green box reappears indicating the device is reachable and running normally.
3. In QPM, the "Status:" field reflects the device is back online.
4. Sensor data resumes flowing without manual intervention.
IWS - Verify multiple IWS devices appear in Configurator
2. Multiple IWS devices (at least 2) are powered on and connected to the same network as the Q-SYS Core.
2. Review the discovered devices list.
Preconditions: 1. All IWS devices on the network automatically appear in Configurator.

Title: 2. Each device shows a unique identifier (name, IP address, serial number, or MAC address).
3. Devices can be individually selected.
